data.title: Why Is Energy Vault Stock Falling Thursday?
data.description:
Energy Vault Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:NRGV) stock fell Thursday following the company’s upsized convertible notes offering that raised dilution concerns. The pullback comes despite strength in the broader market, suggesting the move is largely driven by deal-specific factors.

Upsized Convertible Notes Offering Weighs On Shares

The company priced $140 million of 5.25% convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private placement, upsized from $125 million, with an option for purchasers to buy an additional $20 million.

While the offering strengthens the company’s liquidity, shares are under pressure as convertible debt offerings can raise concerns about future dilution. Such deals can also create short-term selling pressure related to hedging activity by note purchasers.

Net proceeds are expected to be approximately $135.5 million, or up to $154.8 million if the option is fully exercised. The company said it plans to use the proceeds for capped call transactions, debt repayment, and general corporate purposes.

Preliminary Q4 Revenue Outlook Improves Sharply

The company issued preliminary guidance for both the fourth quarter and the full fiscal year 2025.

The company now expects fourth quarter sales of $150 million to $155 million, above the Street estimate of $140.86 million. This compares with $33.47 million in revenue a year ago, reflecting a sharp year-over-year increase.

For the full year, Energy Vault sees fiscal 2025 sales of $200 million to $205 million, topping analyst expectations of $191.24 million.

Energy Vault also highlighted its footprint across the United States, China and other regions, with the majority of revenue generated domestically. The company continues positioning itself to benefit from the growing demand for renewable energy storage solutions.

Crusoe Deal Expands Into AI Data Center Infrastructure

The broader market rose on the previous trading day, with the S&P 500 rising 0.35% and the Nasdaq gaining 0.35%. Energy Vault’s decline contrasts with this positive market sentiment, indicating that specific challenges may be weighing on investor confidence.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 33.3% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 18.8% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a bearish trend in the short- to medium-term. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 161.94%, reflecting strong longer-term performance, and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 38.22, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $3.50
  • Key Support: $3.00.

This positioning is particularly relevant given the recent financial estimates, as the company seeks to enhance its market share in the renewable energy sector. As the demand for sustainable energy solutions continues to grow, Energy Vault’s innovative approach to energy storage could play a significant role in shaping the future of energy infrastructure.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Energy Vault Holdings, Inc. is slated to provide its next financial update on March 17, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 4 cents (Up from Loss of 43 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $136.68 million (Up from $33.47 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $5.39. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Goldman Sachs: Sell (Raises Target to $2.00) (Nov. 12, 2025)
  • Goldman Sachs: Sell (Raises Target to $1.50) (Oct. 8, 2025)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Energy Vault Holdings, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Strong (Score: 98.19) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Energy Vault Holdings’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, indicating that the stock is currently outperforming the broader market. However, investors should remain cautious given the recent financial outlook and potential challenges ahead.

Top ETF Exposure

  • Invesco WilderHill Clean Energy ETF (NYSE:PBW): 1.72% Weight
  • Proshares S&P Kensho Cleantech ETF (NYSE:CTEX): 4.50% Weight

Significance: Because NRGV carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

NRGV Price Action: Energy Vault Holdings shares were down 20.69% at $3.22 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Rivian Stock Edges Lower Before Q4 Results
data.description:
Rivian Automotive Inc (NASDAQ:RIVN) shares are trading lower Thursday morning as investors position ahead of the electric-vehicle maker's fourth-quarter earnings report, due after the closing bell. Here's what investors should be watching closely.

  • RIvian stock is down Thursday. What’s ahead for RIVN stock?

Q4 Expectations: Smaller Per-Share Loss, Stronger Sales

Wall Street is looking for Rivian to post a fourth-quarter loss of 46 cents per share, narrowing from a loss of 81 cents a year earlier. On the top line, consensus calls for revenue of about $1.73 billion, up from roughly $1.26 billion in the prior-year quarter.

The estimates suggest analysts expect Rivian's production ramp and cost-cutting efforts to keep improving even as overall EV demand cools.

Looking Back At Recent Momentum

The company has since agreed to pay $250 million to settle a lawsuit tied to its 2021 IPO, while advancing custom silicon, autonomy and a major software partnership with Volkswagen—steps that clear legal overhang and highlight its tech ambitions.

What To Listen For After The Bell

On tonight's call, traders will focus on Rivian's 2026 production outlook, margin trajectory and cash burn, plus fresh detail on the mass-market R2 crossover and any additional variants.

Commentary on demand trends, pricing and the competitive landscape versus Tesla and legacy automakers could determine RIVN's next move.

Rivian’s Business Model

Rivian is a battery electric vehicle automaker that sells its vehicles in the US and Canada. The company also develops electronic control units and related software for autos in a joint venture with Volkswagen. Rivian has multiple vehicles in its fleet, which include a luxury truck and full-size SUV and a delivery van. Rivian is also developing autonomous driving software to be used in its vehicles.

The recent reviews of the R2 model are significant as they may influence consumer interest and sales, especially as Rivian competes in a market dominated by established players like Tesla.

Rivian Analyst Ratings And Targets

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $16.25. Recent analyst moves include:

  • UBS: Downgraded to Sell (Raises Target to $15.00) (Jan. 14)
  • Wolfe Research: Downgraded to Underperform (Jan. 12)
  • Piper Sandler: Neutral (Raises Target to $20.00) (Jan. 8)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Rivian Automotive, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Strong (Score: 70.97) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Rivian Automotive’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, indicating that the stock is currently outperforming the broader market. Investors should monitor upcoming earnings and analyst sentiment as the company continues to develop its product lineup and navigate competitive pressures.

RIVN Shares Fall Thursday

RIVN Price Action: Rivian Automotive shares were down 3.59% at $14.24 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Viking Therapeutics Stock Soars: What's Driving The Momentum?
data.description:
Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VKTX) stock is soaring on Thursday, extending the momentum it picked up in Wednesday's after‑hours session following the company's fourth‑quarter earnings release.

  • Viking Therapeutics shares are showing limited movement. Where is VKTX stock headed?

What The Numbers Showed

Viking posted a loss of $1.38 per share, worse than the 89 cent loss analysts were expecting. Last quarter, the company only missed on EPS by 13 cents, according to Benzinga Pro.

The miss was driven by a sharp increase in research and development spending, which jumped to $153.5 million, up from $31 million a year earlier.

CEO Brian Lian put the quarter into perspective, noting that "the past year was an exceptional year for Viking, marked by rapid progress across our obesity portfolio."

VK2735 Advances On Both Injectable And Oral Fronts

The company's Phase 3 VANQUISH program for its subcutaneous VK2735 obesity drug is moving quickly, with the goal of being used for the treatment of obesity and other metabolic disorders. Enrollment for VANQUISH‑1 is already complete and VANQUISH‑2 is close behind. Data from that study is expected in the third quarter of 2026.

Additionally, progress on the oral version of VK2735 appears to be driving shares higher. Viking confirmed that its tablet formulation is on track to enter Phase 3 trials in the third quarter of 2026.

Having both an injectable and an oral option built around the same active ingredient could give Viking a meaningful edge, as no other dual or triple agonist in development currently exists in both forms. It also means patients could switch between the two without the added uncertainty that comes with changing to an entirely different therapy.

What Do The Technicals Show?

Viking Therapeutics is currently showing a mixed technical picture, with its stock trading above the 20-day SMA but below the 50-day and 100-day SMAs. This setup indicates a potential struggle for momentum, as the stock is navigating through a complex trend environment.

The RSI is currently at 40.61, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting the stock is neither overbought nor oversold, indicating a lack of strong momentum in either direction at this time. MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on Viking’s stock.

Key support is at $29.50, while resistance sits at $36.00. If the stock approaches support and fails to hold, it could signal further downside, while a breakout above resistance may indicate a bullish trend continuation.

Over the past 12 months, its stock has gained 11.79%, reflecting a generally positive longer-term trend despite recent volatility. The stock has managed to maintain an upward trajectory over the year, even when there has been challenges.

VKTX Price Action: Viking Therapeutics shares were up 9.56% at $31.28 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Here's How Much $100 Invested In Baidu 20 Years Ago Would Be Worth Today
data.description:
Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) has outperformed the market over the past 20 years by 13.3% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 22.03%. Currently, Baidu has a market capitalization of $50.49 billion.

Buying $100 In BIDU: If an investor had bought $100 of BIDU stock 20 years ago, it would be worth $5,362.74 today based on a price of $143.99 for BIDU at the time of writing.

Baidu's Performance Over Last 20 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

data.title: Trump-Affiliated New Forex Platform 'World Swap' Sends WLFI Soaring 6%
data.description:
Trump's World Liberty Financial (CRYPTO: WLFI) rose 6% on Thursday after announcing World Swap, a forex and remittance platform targeting the $7 trillion global currency exchange market.

The World Swap Launch

Co-founder Zak Folkman told attendees at Consensus Hong Kong that World Swap will connect users directly to debit cards and bank accounts worldwide, settling foreign exchange remittances at “a fraction of” competitor fees.

“There’s over $7 trillion of money moving around the world from currency to currency, and all of this has been taxed very heavily by the incumbent players,” Folkman said.

The platform operates within the USD1 stablecoin ecosystem.

World Liberty Markets, the firm’s lending platform launched four weeks ago, has already recorded $320 million in lending and over $200 million borrowed.

More products are coming. World Liberty Financial partnered with Spacecoin last month to build a token swap system using USD1. 

Spacecoin launched three satellites into orbit as part of its low-Earth-orbit network, with the new technology utilizing WLFI’s financial system.

Additional launches include real-world asset products using USD1 as collateral to expose institutional investors, plus a WLFI debit card for day-to-day activities. 

Further announcements are expected at a Mar-a-Lago event later this month.

The Conflict Question

World Liberty fueled sharp income increases for the Trump Organization, including from foreign entities, in the first half of last year, Reuters reported in October. 

Government ethics experts say the Trump family’s crypto initiatives constitute a conflict of interest while Trump oversees U.S. crypto policy. 

However, the White House denies any conflicts exist.

WLFI Technical Setup

WLFI is currently bouncing from critical $0.09-$0.10 support.

The Parabolic SAR at $0.11816 sits just above price, indicating bearish territory but close to potential reversal if WLFI breaks above.

The RSI at 32.60 sits in oversold territory below 30, suggesting recent selling was excessive and a technical bounce is reasonable. 

Immediate resistance appears at $0.11-$0.12 where the SAR and previous support converge. 

Above that, $0.14-$0.15 represents more substantial resistance, followed by $0.16-$0.18 where the token consolidated in late 2025 and early 2026.

For bulls, WLFI needs to hold above $0.10 and break decisively above $0.12 with the SAR flipping bullish. Failure to hold $0.10 would trigger selling with no clear support structure below.

Image: Shutterstock
